title: Inference-Time Alignment Control for Diffusion Models with Reinforcement Learning Guidance
abstract: Introduces Reinforcement Learning Guidance (RLG), an inference-time enhancement technique for diffusion models that adapts Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) to control alignment with downstream objectives, providing a practical and theoretically sound solution for enhancing and controlling diffusion model alignment at inference.
key_contributions: Provides a practical and theoretically sound solution for enhancing and controlling diffusion model alignment at inference
Addresses limitations of existing RL methods for diffusion models

methodology.approach: Interprets RL fine-tuning of diffusion models as generation conditioned on implicit rewards
methodology.technique: Adapts Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) for alignment control
methodology.timing: Works at inference time rather than requiring fine-tuning

results.qualitative_results: Shows qualitative results for human preference alignment task using SD3.5-M with GRPO and RLG
results.observations: As RLG scale increases, images become more detailed and aesthetically pleasing with rising PickScores

limitations: Intractable probabilities due to diffusion algorithms
